# Project Charter

<!-- Generated by `spec-kitty charter generate` -->

Generated: 2026-07-22T21:09:38Z

## Testing Standards

- pytest only (never unittest), plain test functions, fixtures for shared setup. Tests live in /tests mirroring the source layout. Unit tests fake NATS where practical; a live round-trip integration test runs against real JetStream and is gated behind AGENT_MAIL_INTEGRATION=1. Prefer integration coverage for v1 work. No fixed coverage floor is mandated for this POC-stage project.


## Quality Gates

- Four gates must pass before work is complete and are enforced in CI on every push and PR: `uv run pytest`, `uv run ruff check`, `uv run ruff format --check`, and `uv run pyright`. Prefer auto-fix (ruff --fix) over disabling a rule. Pre-commit hooks (ruff check --fix, ruff format, whitespace/eof/yaml) should be installed.


## Performance Benchmarks

- No hard latency targets; this is a low-throughput coordination tool. Transient IO (NATS connect) must retry with tenacity rather than fail on a cold server.


## Branch Strategy

- Trunk-based on `main`; CI must be green (all four gates) before merge. Conventional commit prefixes (feat/fix/docs/test/chore). Commit and push eagerly in small, focused steps. Changes to public behaviour must update docs in the same change.

- Deployment constraints: Releasable via PyPI (Trusted Publishing / OIDC on v* tags) and as a multi-arch Docker image pushed to GHCR (:latest on main, :version on tags). NATS with JetStream is a runtime prerequisite (not bundled); point NATS_URL at it. The hosted MCP server exposes GET /health. No secrets in the repo; a .env.example documents required keys.


## Governance Activation

```yaml

```

## Policy Summary

- Intent: agent-mail is generic, releasable, open-source infrastructure (GPL-3.0-or-later, published to PyPI and as a Docker image on GHCR): a NATS JetStream-backed mailbox that lets local LLM coding agents (Claude, Codex, Gemini, ...) message and notify each other. It ships a CLI primitive (send/inbox/read/reply/notify) and a hostable MCP server sharing one core. The hosted HTTP server is multi-tenant: each agent connects on its own URL (/<agent>/mcp) which is its whole identity and configuration. It must remain generic — no deployment-specific hostnames, IPs, secrets, or organisation names in code, docs, or tests; agent names are config.

- Languages/Frameworks: Python 3.12+ only, managed exclusively with uv (uv sync / uv run / uv add). Libraries: click (CLI), pydantic + pydantic-settings (models/config), nats-py (JetStream), mcp / FastMCP (MCP server), tenacity (retries), uvicorn (HTTP). Build backend hatchling + hatch-vcs (version from git tags).

- Testing: pytest only (never unittest), plain test functions, fixtures for shared setup. Tests live in /tests mirroring the source layout. Unit tests fake NATS where practical; a live round-trip integration test runs against real JetStream and is gated behind AGENT_MAIL_INTEGRATION=1. Prefer integration coverage for v1 work. No fixed coverage floor is mandated for this POC-stage project.

- Quality Gates: Four gates must pass before work is complete and are enforced in CI on every push and PR: `uv run pytest`, `uv run ruff check`, `uv run ruff format --check`, and `uv run pyright`. Prefer auto-fix (ruff --fix) over disabling a rule. Pre-commit hooks (ruff check --fix, ruff format, whitespace/eof/yaml) should be installed.

- Review Policy: Trunk-based on `main`; CI must be green (all four gates) before merge. Conventional commit prefixes (feat/fix/docs/test/chore). Commit and push eagerly in small, focused steps. Changes to public behaviour must update docs in the same change.

- Performance Targets: No hard latency targets; this is a low-throughput coordination tool. Transient IO (NATS connect) must retry with tenacity rather than fail on a cold server.

- Deployment Constraints: Releasable via PyPI (Trusted Publishing / OIDC on v* tags) and as a multi-arch Docker image pushed to GHCR (:latest on main, :version on tags). NATS with JetStream is a runtime prerequisite (not bundled); point NATS_URL at it. The hosted MCP server exposes GET /health. No secrets in the repo; a .env.example documents required keys.


## Project Directives

1. Respect risk boundaries: Most costly failures: silently dropping or double-processing a message, and leaking deployment-specific or secret material into the public repo. Durability and idempotency are JetStream's job (durable per-agent consumers, explicit ack/nak) — a redelivered message must never be double-processed. The CLI and MCP server must both delegate to the single core (agent_mail.mailbox.Mailbox); no logic duplication. Follow the EUNRG coding standards (docs/coding-standards.md): full type annotations, absolute imports, specific exceptions from a project-level exceptions module (AgentMailError base), logging over print (click.echo allowed for CLI output but also log), config through one pydantic-settings object, frozen immutable data where practical, mature pinned dependencies.

2. Keep documentation synchronized with workflow and behavior changes: README is the front door; docs/ holds the hosting guide, MCP-client setup, the agent-onboarding page, the "check your inbox" snippet, and architecture decisions under docs/decisions (ADRs). docs/coding-standards.md is the canonical coding baseline and AGENTS.md is the project charter summary. Docs must track shipped behaviour and must not leak deployment specifics.

## Amendment Process

Governance changes go through /spec-kitty.charter (edit answers.yaml, regenerate, commit). AGENTS.md mirrors the binding rules for day-to-day agent reference.


## Exception Policy

Deviations from the coding-standards baseline must be documented with their reason in AGENTS.md under project-specific overrides. `except Exception:` is permitted only at process boundaries (CLI main, HTTP handler) as a logging backstop.
